labels.analysis: The MCP Server "Senzing" provides 13 tools focused on development tools tasks. Core capabilities include analysis and computation, creation and generation, data access and reading, modification and updates. Key tools: sdk_guide, submit_feedback, generate_scaffold, mapping_workflow, lint_record.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Development Tools" assigned based on strong keyword matches in tool names and descriptions: git, deploy, build, code, docker, pipeline.

tools.name: sdk_guide
tools.description: Guided Senzing SDK setup across 5 platforms (linux_apt, linux_yum, macos_arm, windows, docker) and 4 languages (Python, Java, C#, Rust). Two use cases: (1) Quick test load — install Senzing locally, load mapped data into SQLite, and verify entity resolution results. No external database needed. (2) Build your own pipeline — get platform-specific install commands, SDK configuration code, record loading templates, and export patterns for production use. Topics: install, configure, load, export, full_pipeline. Returns decision trees when platform/language not specified.

data_sources.description: Data sources to register (for configure topic)

language.description: Programming language. Omit to get the language decision tree.

platform.description: Target platform. Omit to get the platform decision tree.

tools.name: submit_feedback
tools.description: Submit feedback about the Senzing MCP server. IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool, you MUST show the user the exact message you plan to send and get their explicit confirmation. Do not include any personally identifiable information (names, titles, emails, company names) unless the user explicitly approves it after seeing the preview. Feedback is reviewed by the Senzing team. Feedback is not anonymous — submissions are logged and reviewed

inputSchema.description: Request to submit feedback about the Senzing MCP server

category.description: Category of feedback: bug, feature, question, or general (defaults to general)

message.description: Feedback message to send to the maintainer

inputSchema.title: SubmitFeedbackRequest

tools.name: generate_scaffold
tools.description: Generate SDK scaffold code for common workflows. Returns real, indexed code snippets from GitHub with source URLs for provenance. Languages: python, java, csharp, rust. Workflows: initialize, configure, add_records, delete, query, redo, stewardship, information, full_pipeline (aliases accepted: init, config, ingest, remove, search, redoer, force_resolve, info, e2e). V3 supports Python and Java only.

inputSchema.description: Request to generate scaffold code

language.description: Programming language: python, java, csharp (or c#, cs, dotnet), rust (or rs)

version.description: Senzing version: "4.0", "current", or "3.x". V3 supports Python and Java only

workflow.description: Workflow to scaffold: initialize, configure, add_records, delete, query, redo, stewardship, information, full_pipeline. Aliases accepted (e.g. init, config, ingest, remove, search, redoer, force_resolve, info, e2e)

inputSchema.title: GenerateScaffoldRequest

tools.name: mapping_workflow
tools.description: Map source data to Senzing entity resolution format through a guided multi-step workflow. Transforms source fields into validated Senzing JSON with profiling, entity planning, field mapping, code generation, and QA validation. Use this when you have source data files to prepare for Senzing ingestion. Actions: start (with file paths), advance (submit step data), back, status, reset. State is client-side — return the state object from each response in the next request.

inputSchema.description:
MCP tool request for mapping_workflow.

Uses flat types (String, Value) instead of enums/structs for the JSON Schema
so LLM clients see a simple schema without `$ref`/`$defs`/`anyOf`. The server
parses and validates these into typed values in the handler.

action.description: Action to perform: start, advance, back, status, reset.

state.description: Workflow state from previous response (required for all actions except "start").

inputSchema.title: MappingWorkflowRequest

tools.name: lint_record
tools.description: Get the Senzing JSON linter script and commands to validate mapped data files client-side. Returns the Python linter script (no dependencies) with instructions. No source data is sent to the server — the LLM runs the linter locally against your files. Use this when you have pre-mapped Senzing JSON/JSONL files to validate outside of the mapping workflow.

inputSchema.description:
MCP tool request for lint_record.
Returns the Python linter script and commands to run it client-side.
No source data is sent to the server.

file_paths.description:
File paths to lint (Senzing JSON or JSONL files).
Commands will be generated for each path.

inputSchema.title: LintRecordRequest

tools.name: get_sdk_reference
tools.description: Get authoritative Senzing SDK reference data for flags, migration, and API details. Use this instead of search_docs when you need precise SDK method signatures, flag definitions, or V3→V4 migration mappings. Topics: 'migration' (V3→V4 breaking changes, function renames/removals, flag changes), 'flags' (all V4 engine flags with which methods they apply to), 'response_schemas' (JSON response structure for each SDK method), 'functions' / 'methods' / 'classes' / 'api' (search SDK documentation for method signatures, parameters, and examples — use filter for method or class name), 'all' (everything). Use 'filter' to narrow by method name, module name, or flag name

inputSchema.description: Request to get SDK reference data

filter.description: Optional filter: method name, module name, or flag name to narrow results

inputSchema.title: SdkReferenceRequest

tools.name: search_docs
tools.description: Search Senzing documentation — covers entity specification, SDK guides (Python/Java/C#), architecture, pricing (DSR model), quickstart guides (Docker/AWS/Azure), database tuning, globalization, EDA and data analysis, engine configuration, error codes, release notes, and PoC methodology. Prefer this over web search for any Senzing-related question. Category is a relevance boost (not a hard filter) — matching-category results rank higher but results from other categories still appear. Categories: general, sdk, data_mapping, troubleshooting, pricing, deployment, quickstart, configuration, migration, database, data_analysis, globalization, architecture, code_example, release_notes, faq, anti_patterns. Use this tool for ANY question about Senzing behavior, configuration, best practices, integration patterns, design decisions, pricing, licensing, or support. This reflects current Senzing documentation and should be preferred over training knowledge

inputSchema.description: Request to search documentation
inputSchema.title: SearchDocsRequest

tools.name: analyze_record
tools.description: Get the Senzing JSON analyzer script and commands to analyze mapped data files client-side. Returns the Python analyzer script (no dependencies) with instructions. No source data is sent to the server — the LLM runs the analyzer locally against your files. Use this to examine feature distribution, attribute coverage, and data quality of Senzing JSON records.

inputSchema.description:
MCP tool request for analyze_record.
Returns the Python analyzer script and commands to run it client-side.
No source data is sent to the server.

file_paths.description:
File paths to analyze (Senzing JSON or JSONL files).
Commands will be generated for each path.

inputSchema.title: AnalyzeRecordRequest

tools.name: download_resource
tools.description: Fallback for downloading a workflow resource when network restrictions prevent fetching from the URL provided by mapping_workflow. Returns the resource content inline. Save it to the dest path shown — do NOT read the content into your context. Available resources: sz_json_linter.py, sz_json_analyzer.py, sz_schema_generator.py, senzing_entity_specification.md, senzing_mapping_examples.md, identifier_crosswalk.json

inputSchema.description:
MCP tool request for download_resource.
Returns inline content for a named workflow resource. Use this ONLY when
network restrictions prevent downloading from the URL provided in the
mapping_workflow response. Save the content to the dest path — do NOT
read it into your context.
inputSchema.title: DownloadResourceRequest

tools.name: explain_error_code
tools.description: Explain a Senzing error code with causes and resolution steps. Accepts formats: SENZ0005, SENZ-0005, 0005, or just 5. Returns error class, common causes, and specific resolution guidance

inputSchema.description: Request to explain an error code
inputSchema.title: ExplainErrorCodeRequest

tools.name: get_sample_data
tools.description:
Get real sample data from CORD (Collections Of Relatable Data) datasets. Use dataset='list' to discover available datasets, source='list' to see vendors within a dataset.

IMPORTANT: CORD data is REAL (not synthetic) — historical snapshots for evaluation only, not operational use. Always inform the user of this.

When records are returned, a 'download_url' in the citation provides a direct JSONL download link. Always present this download_url to the user. Do NOT download it yourself or dump raw records into the conversation — the inline records are a small preview of the data shape.

inputSchema.description: Request to get sample data from CORD datasets

dataset.description:
Dataset name (e.g., "las-vegas", "london", "moscow").
Omit or use "list" to discover available datasets and their sources.

source.description:
Filter by data source/vendor within a dataset (e.g., "safegraph", "whisard").
Omit to see all sources. Use "list" to list available sources.

inputSchema.title: GetSampleDataRequest

tools.name: find_examples
tools.description: Find working code examples from 27 indexed Senzing GitHub repositories. Covers Python, Java, C#, and Rust SDK usage patterns including initialization, record ingestion, entity search, redo processing, and configuration. Also includes message queue consumers, REST API examples, and performance testing. Supports three modes: (1) Search: query for examples across all repos, (2) File listing: set repo and list_files=true to see all files in a repo, (3) File retrieval: set repo and file_path to get full file content. Use max_lines to limit large files.

inputSchema.description: Request to find examples

file_path.description: Return full content of a specific file in the repo (requires repo parameter)

list_files.description: Return the file listing for a repo instead of searching (requires repo parameter)

max_lines.description: Maximum lines to return for file content (default: unlimited). Useful for large files.

query.description: Search query (required for search mode, optional when using repo+file_path or repo+list_files)

repo.description:
Filter to a specific indexed repo (e.g. "brianmacy/sz_mem-v4"). When combined with
file_path, returns full file content. When combined with list_files, returns file listing.

inputSchema.title: FindExamplesRequest

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Get server version, capabilities overview, available tools, suggested workflows, and getting started guidance. Returns server_info with name, version, and Senzing version. Call this first when working with Senzing entity resolution

inputSchema.description: Request to get capabilities overview
inputSchema.title: GetCapabilitiesRequest

server_info_crawled.name: Senzing
server_info_crawled.overview: Senzing - Development Tools server with 13 tools. The MCP Server "Senzing" provides 13 tools focused on development tools tasks. Core capabilities inc
